ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific term for blended iced chocolate coffee frappuccino; 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301710) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific frappuccino or blended iced coffee sub-code exists. This is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for blended iced chocolate coffee frappuccino without whipped cream.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one medium cup of approximately 350 ml, equivalent to roughly 350 g, as commonly served at specialty coffee outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate Frappuccino (No Whipped Cream) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chocolate Frappuccino (No Whipped Cream), cr_id D08020003, with confidence 0.95. All per-100g values are taken as reported.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Milk-Based Coffee Beverages — Analogous Estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (Chocolate Milk and Iced Coffee Beverage entries)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a chocolate frappuccino without whipped cream in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 40 is estimated by analogy to chocolate milk (GI ~34–43, University of Sydney GI Database) and iced coffee with milk and sugar (GI ~38–42). The milk protein and fat content moderate the glycemic response despite the sugar load. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 29.4) / 100 ≈ 11.8, rounded to 11.9. This estimate is consistent with the input record's embedded GI value of 40 (low). Confidence for this GI estimate is moderate given the analogous basis.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.6 g sugar per 100 g; dietary sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage as it qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (7.6 g sugar/100 g, exceeding the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold); added dietary sugar independently raises blood pressure risk regardless of the low sodium content (28 mg/100 g), per AHA 2018 guidance — request sugar-free chocolate syrup or choose unsweetened iced coffee instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.6 g sugar per 100 g and near-zero dietary fibre (0.09 g/100 g); liquid sugar raises postprandial glucose rapidly
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this drink as it is a sugar-sweetened beverage delivering 7.6 g of sugar per 100 g with negligible fibre (0.09 g/100 g), causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation; if a chocolate coffee drink is desired, request sugar-free chocolate syrup, unsweetened milk, and confirm no added syrups.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content (4.69 g/100 g, predominantly saturated) combined with added sugar load in a liquid format
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this beverage presents a moderate concern due to its saturated fat content (~2.9 g/100 g estimated) and high sugar load (7.6 g/100 g) in liquid form, which contributes to dyslipidaemia and insulin resistance risk; limit consumption and prefer plain iced coffee with low-fat unsweetened milk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (7.6 g/100 g) with near-zero fibre negates potential coffee polyphenol anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For individuals with rheumatoid arthritis, the added sugar in this frappuccino (7.6 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory pathways and negates any anti-inflammatory benefit from coffee polyphenols; request sugar-free syrup and plain milk to obtain the polyphenol benefit without the inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chocolate Frappuccino (No Whipped Cream) is a blended iced coffee beverage made from espresso or strong coffee, chocolate syrup, whole or semi-skimmed milk, and crushed ice, served without whipped cream. The drink is smooth, cold, and moderately sweet with a pronounced chocolate-coffee flavour and a creamy texture derived from the milk base. It is a popular specialty coffee shop item widely available in Singapore and internationally. Per 100 g, it provides 83 kcal, 2.35 g protein, 4.69 g fat, 8.39 g carbohydrate, 7.6 g sugar, 0.09 g dietary fibre, and 28 mg sodium. The calcium content is 70 mg/100 g, reflecting the milk base. The absence of whipped cream reduces total fat compared to the standard version but does not materially change the sugar-sweetened beverage classification. Clinically, this drink is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to its sugar content exceeding 5 g/100 g, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without modification. The glycemic index is estimated at 40 (low) by analogy to chocolate milk and iced coffee beverages, but the glycemic load per medium serving (~350 g) is approximately 11.9, which is clinically meaningful. The drink contains milk and is therefore not suitable for those with dairy allergies or lactose intolerance.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D08020003) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (moderate confidence for GI component only); all other values are exact from HPB.
notes: Saturated fat estimated at approximately 2.9 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central reference for similar chocolate milk-based blended coffee beverages (USDA SR Legacy item 'Beverages, coffee, mocha, ready-to-drink'), as HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food item and are not available from the primary approved source for this specific preparation. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on chocolate milk and iced coffee beverage entries; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the approved GI database. GL per serving calculated as (40 × 29.4 g carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 ≈ 11.8, reported as 11.9. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; it is an internationally available Western specialty coffee beverage, so no mandatory non-English local-language names are required under the multilingual naming rules.
